Patch Set 5:

Serguei, I've landed this patch to master, can you please cherry-pick to b2_14, b_es6_0, maybe also b_es5_2 and b2_12 if they are affected.

Patch Set 5:

Andreas, I didn't find similar kind of failures in the branches you mention. The issue appears to have been introduced by f9669c4d309 
 "LU-14990 tests: Detect correct LNet interface for sanity-lnet" and which hasn't been ported. (I should've checked this earlier so I could add the "Fixes" line to the commit).

Patch Set 5:

Hi, thanks for getting this fixed on master!  Is there a reason that LU-15210 is private?

Patch Set 5:

Looks like at some point LU-15210 was turned into DCO-8815. I'm not sure why, perhaps Minh can explain.

Patch Set 5:

> Looks like at some point LU-15210 was turned into DCO-8815. I'm not
 > sure why, perhaps Minh can explain.

The DCO project is used for tickets related to the internal test hardware.  We were hoping this could be resolved with a configuration change on the new test hardware to remove the second address, but it is easier/better to fix this in the test scripts.

Unfortunately, Jira doesn't allow moving a ticket back to the original project and preserving the *same* issue number, so it now appears as LU-15232, but will at least redirect from LU-15210 properly.

parents.subject: LU-13456 ldlm: fix reprocessing of locks with more bits

commit.subject: LU-15210 tests: fix sanity-lnet to handle duplicate IP
commit.message:
LU-15210 tests: fix sanity-lnet to handle duplicate IP

The same IP may be added on the same interface with different netmasks
specified.
sanity-lnet is not expecting this and fails. Fix it.

Test-parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet

Signed-off-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I70c2df5f14c88362ea5af6f06410823c56535dee
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/45551
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
